name: Peppery Hamburgers W/Red Wine Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Freshly ground pepper or coarsely cracked black peppercorns
1/4 cup Fresh or canned chicken broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the meat in a mixing bowl.
2. Heat the oil in a small cast-iron skillet over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring until wilted.
3. Add the garlic and cook briefly, stirring. Do not let the garlic burn. Remove from the heat and let cool.
4. Add the cooled onion mixture, chili powder, cumin, ground pepper, red pepper flakes, Worcestershire sauce, and salt to the meat. Blend well with a spatula until all ingredients are incorporated.
5. Divide the mixture into 4 patties of equal size.
6. Heat a heavy cast-iron skillet large enough to hold all the patties in one layer over medium heat. Do not add any additional fat.
7. When the skillet is hot and smoking, add the patties. Cook until well browned on one side, then turn and cook 3 minutes more on the second side for medium rare. Cook longer if desired.
8. Transfer the patties to a warm plate.
9. In a small skillet over medium heat, melt the butter. Add the shallots and cook, stirring for 1 minute.
10. Add the wine and broth, then reduce by about 1/4 cup, stirring occasionally.
11. Check for seasoning and sprinkle with parsley before serving. Serve immediately.
